_readme.what_this_is: Archetype MANIFEST for an AI/LLM application — chatbots, RAG, assistants, agents, or an AI feature inside a larger product. Resolve with `node library/resolve.mjs archetype.ai-app.json`.
_readme.reuse: Five AI-specific checkpoints (ai.*) carry the LLM backbone — prompt injection, output handling, tool-use safety, cost controls, data boundary. Everything else is COMPOSED from the shared library: an AI app still needs the universal security spine (secrets for provider keys, error handling for streaming/provider failures, PII, abuse controls, access control), so those are reused, not re-authored.
_readme.why_these_criticals: prompt_injection and output_handling apply to every LLM app (input -> model -> output). tool_use_safety is critical wherever the model can act; on a pure chatbot with no tools it correctly reads as not-applicable (and surfaces as a gap to build if you add tools). secrets_management is critical because an AI app is defined by holding a provider key.
_readme.how_scoring_works: Levels 3/6/9. 3 = crude/risky (the vulnerability is present). 6 = solid, shippable (the property holds). 9 = great. TRUST THE GAP, NOT THE NUMBER.

applies_when: An app whose core is calling an LLM: a chatbot, RAG/assistant, agent, or an AI feature in a larger product. Untrusted user (and often retrieved or tool) input reaches a model, the model's output is used, and the model may be able to call tools. A provider API key and often user data cross to a third party.

goal_definition.honest_ceiling: The ai.* checkpoints grade the code paths that reach the model; a full red-team of prompt injection needs adversarial testing the static rubric can flag the absence of but cannot itself perform.
